data_window_note: MMS L2 data ~90 days behind real-time

unusual_events.explanation: Unusually large magnetic field: B_max = 55.4 nT (4.3σ above mean). Possible causes: magnetospheric compression, flux tube boundary, current sheet crossing, or reconnection inflow region.

20 electron holes (bipolar E∥ events) detected in 6 hours. This is unusually high activity, indicating an active or recently saturated two-stream instability. These phase-space vortices are the direct nonlinear saturation products of Langmuir wave trapping (Bernstein et al. 1957, Graham et al. 2016 GRL).

electron_holes.note: 20 bipolar E∥ events above 3.97 mV/m — consistent with nonlinear TSI saturation products (electron holes/phase-space vortices)

electrons.beam_note: No clear beam — mean Ve=184.0 km/s < 300 km/s threshold.
electrons.dfdv_note: v_beam/v_th = 0.04 < 1.5 → no bump-on-tail. Beam insufficient for classical TSI.

theory_validation.validation_note: Beam velocity too low for classical TSI at observed conditions.

tsi_weibel_chain.note: Chain not detected — no electron beam present

langmuir_proxy.note: f_pe=35.17 kHz outside EDP frequency range (Nyquist ~16 Hz for slow survey)
